surface_map.notes: No export/import session actions in desktop-menu.ts
Session export/import CLI commands were recently deleted
No export/import endpoints in SDK. fork/share/unshare are remote only.
Natural host for export/import. @zip.js/zip.js already used in logging.ts for export-debug-logs.
Contains shared session-message, session-prompt, session-event types

existing_fork_share_patterns.name: DialogFork
existing_fork_share_patterns.pattern: SolidJS Component, uses @opencode-ai/ui/dialog + List, lazy-imported via dialog.show(() => <DialogFork />)
existing_fork_share_patterns.notes: Does NOT exist yet for export. Fork creates new session on server; does not produce a file.

existing_fork_share_patterns.name: Session Share
existing_fork_share_patterns.pattern: Inline share/unshare functions, not a dialog. SDK client calls for remote share.
existing_fork_share_patterns.backend: packages/opencode/src/share/share-next.ts — connects to opncd.ai enterprise server
existing_fork_share_patterns.notes: Remote-only. No dialog component exists. Share creates URL, doesn't export file.

existing_fork_share_patterns.name: Export Debug Logs
existing_fork_share_patterns.pattern: Creates ZIP archive using @zip.js/zip.js, writes to app.getPath('downloads'), opens folder with shell.showItemInFolder
existing_fork_share_patterns.notes: Closest existing pattern to session export — reuse @zip.js/zip.js and download folder reveal pattern.

dialog_ui_pattern.closing: dialog.close() — called inside dialog via handleSelect/handleSave
dialog_ui_pattern.props: Dialog title, size ('x-large'), transition, fit, description

file_picker_types.openFilePickerDialog: OpenFilePickerOptions = { title?: string; multiple?: boolean; accept?: string[]; extensions?: string[] }

desktop_ipc.open-file-picker: packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts:169 — dialog.showOpenDialog, returns filePaths | string | null
desktop_ipc.open-directory-picker: packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts:153 — dialog.showOpenDialog, returns filePaths | string | null

key_types.Session.Info: packages/opencode/src/session/session.ts:208 — Schema.Struct with ~17 fields: id, title, version, model, provider, time_created, time_updated, share_url, summary, revert, permission, project_id, workspace_id, child_session_id, archived, agent, usage
key_types.Part: TextPart, FilePart, ToolPart, ReasoningPart, StepStartPart, StepFinishPart, SnapshotPart, PatchPart, AgentPart, RetryPart, CompactionPart, SubtaskPart

session_data_model.serialization: fromRow/ toRow — manual field mapping between Drizzle rows and Info objects

key_types.State: packages/app/src/context/global-sync/types.ts:33 — massive store type with session[], message{}, part{}, permission{}, todo{}, etc.
key_types.Session (SDK type): packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/types.gen.ts:751 — includes id, project_id, title, created_at, updated_at, model, provider, agent, share, summary, revert, context_window, etc.

session_data_model.storage: localStorage via @solid-primitives/storage, managed by persist.ts with LRU cache
session_data_model.deserialization: Schema-driven on server (Effect Schema), localStorage parse on client

security_sanitization_concerns.concern: API keys in tool call outputs
security_sanitization_concerns.current_pattern: No centralized sanitizer; ad-hoc in provider/transform.ts
security_sanitization_concerns.recommendation: Strip API keys, tokens, and secrets from tool call outputs before export

security_sanitization_concerns.current_pattern: Path encoding via base64Encode in dialog-select-file.tsx
security_sanitization_concerns.recommendation: Consider whether local file paths should be preserved or redacted in exported sessions

security_sanitization_concerns.current_pattern: No existing session export sanitization — this is greenfield
security_sanitization_concerns.recommendation: Define a SessionExportSchema that excludes sensitive fields (permissions, API keys in tool results)

security_sanitization_concerns.located: packages/app/src/context/permission.tsx — stores auto-accept rules in localStorage
security_sanitization_concerns.recommendation: Permission rules should not be included in exported session data
Consider whether share URLs should be stripped on export since they won't be valid in new context

i18n_touch_points.key_pattern: dot-separated flat keys, namespace prefix: command.*, dialog.*, toast.*, common.*, session.*

test_runner_per_package.packages/opencode: Bun test --timeout 30000 (78+ test files, test/fixture/fixture.ts fixture system)
test_runner_per_package.packages/desktop: No test runner — only 2 test files (html, shell-env)

httpsession_api_test.httpapi-session.test.ts: 913 lines — CRUD, fork, message mutation, cursor pagination, error shapes, directory/workspace scoping
httpsession_api_test.fork_coverage: httpapi-session.test.ts:641-671 tests POST /session/:id/fork with content-type handling and error shapes
httpsession_api_test.share_coverage: share-next.test.ts:331 lines — mocked HTTP only, no integration test against real share API
httpsession_api_test.import_coverage: cli/import.test.ts:54 lines — URL parsing and data transformation only, no end-to-end import flow
missing: No session export/import test at all
No share integration test (mocked HTTP only)
No Postgres integration tests for sessions

opencode_session_test_files: test/session/prompt.test.ts (2345 lines — largest, prompt loop, tool execution, fork)
test/server/httpapi-session.test.ts (913 lines — main HTTP API test)

fixture_system.opencode: test/fixture/fixture.ts — tmpdir(), tmpdirScoped(), provideTmpdirInstance(), TestInstance, disposeAllInstances()
fixture_system.opencode_database: test/fixture/db.ts — resetDatabase() closes DB, deletes SQLite files
fixture_system.opencode_llm_mock: test/lib/llm-server.ts — configurable mock LLM with text/wait/reply helpers
fixture_system.app_e2e_mock: e2e/utils/mock-server.ts — mockOpenCodeServer() intercepts all HTTP routes

test_patterns.layer_mock_pattern: Layer.mock(Service, { method: () => ... }) — throws UnimplementedError for un-stubbed methods
test_patterns.notes: Desktop has critically low test coverage (2 files for 29+ modules). No SDK tests at all. No session serialization tests. Export/import would need: (1) Effect service tests in packages/opencode, (2) IPC handler tests in packages/desktop (requires test setup), (3) dialog component tests in packages/app.

dirty_files_preserved.packages/opencode/src/session/session.ts: M — type annotation fix (1 line diff)

smoking_guns: No export/import feature exists anywhere — this is greenfield development with no pre-existing patterns to extend
SDK has no export/import endpoints — new endpoints must be added or the feature must bypass the SDK and work directly with the opencode service layer
CLI export/import commands were recently deleted (unstaged D files) — check git history at e630a4efc and 69d365932 for prior implementation patterns
Desktop has almost no test infrastructure — 2 test files for 29+ modules means export/import IPC handlers will need new test setup
Session sanitization has no centralized utility — each domain does its own ad-hoc sanitization, so export sanitization needs a utility from scratch
Postgres migration is in-flight — session tables have both SQLite (.sql.ts) and Postgres (.pg.sql.ts) variants; export/import must handle both or pick one
The Platform interface abstracts file dialogs — export won't call Electron IPC directly, it will go through usePlatform().saveFilePickerDialog()
dialog-select-file.tsx uses SDK to list sessions — it imports from pages/session/session-layout and pages/session/helpers, tight coupling to session page routes
